comment.body:
> ### This will not work as expected ❌
> 
>     * We would need to apply this change across all 9 apps
> 
>     * `onSerialReady` in `AbcvlibActivity` is already overridden and is invoked from `MainActivity`.

Understood. That makes sense. Can you be sure to add a comment near the anonymous object implementation briefly describing its function, as I think this will be difficult to understand without further context. 

> * I have explained before that we don't need to modify anything related to the permissions handling.. Kotlin coroutines can make everything work as expected with minor changes.   [PermissionHandlingUpdates: Permissions Handling ANR Issue #14 (comment)](https://github.com/tekkura/sr-android/issues/14#issuecomment-3966306015)

This didn't answer my question. 
Your proposed kotlin version of the fix in https://github.com/tekkura/sr-android/issues/14#issuecomment-3966306015
Does what "Suggested fix 1" from https://github.com/tekkura/sr-android/issues/14#issue-3810091744 does (i.e. it allocates the calls to another thread). What I am asking is why the "Suggested fix 2" is no longer a part of the kotlin solution, and if it is not necessary, then why not. I'm not arguing for it to stay, I just want to understand why it became unnecessary. 

> [@topherbuckley](https://github.com/topherbuckley) I know turning from one solution to another in the same issue is confusing
> So we can close this and move to [#170](https://github.com/tekkura/sr-android/issues/170)

I'd prefer we keep everything related to this in a single issue if possible. I don't see the benefit of starting a new issue that duplicates content from here.
